creator: ade@lovett.com
text:
On Fri, Nov 05, 1999 at 04:31:56AM +0300, Grigoriy Strokin wrote:
> When make is run in the directory /usr/ports/lang/expect, it exists immediately with exit code 0
> and creates work/.build_done as if the port was build. 
> Then `make install' installs nothing but reports about errors.
> What really happens is that configure script fails because it can't find
> the tcl/tk directories.
>
> [snip]

Grigoriy,

It would appear that your ports tree is slightly out of date.

I was responsible for the last update to the port, and rev 1.41 of
the Makefile does indeed DTRT with respect to CONFIGURE_ARGS
looking for tcl/tk 8.2 in the right place.

Can you please check that you have cvsup'd the latest version of
this port, cross-referencing against:

	http://www.FreeBSD.org/cgi/cvsweb.cgi/ports/lang/expect/Makefile

and try again.

Thanks,
	-aDe

-- 
Ade Lovett, Austin, TX.
